shortDescription: Read-only Microsoft Entra and Graph tenant insight via the Microsoft MCP Server for Enterprise.

externalAccountsNeeded.description: Microsoft Entra work or school account in the target tenant

capabilityGroups.shortDescription: Conversational Entra and Microsoft Graph tenant reporting for users, groups, devices, apps, and license posture.
useWhen: you need tenant-wide identity or directory reporting grounded in Microsoft Graph
you want read-only administrative answers about users, groups, applications, devices, or licenses
you need Graph query suggestion before choosing or scripting a concrete endpoint

orchestratorUseCases: answer tenant-level Microsoft Entra and Graph reporting questions without writing custom endpoint logic
suggest and execute read-only Graph queries for identity, app, device, and license investigations
support virtual admin reporting while leaving write-plane automation to native Graph or Exchange execution lanes

maintenanceTasks.name: review-enterprise-mcp-scope-pack
maintenanceTasks.description: Reassess the delegated MCP scope pack when Microsoft expands preview capabilities or when the admin reporting slice changes materially.

tools.name: graphenterprise_suggest_queries
tools.description: Suggest read-only Microsoft Graph queries for Entra and tenant-admin reporting scenarios.
useWhen: you need a safe read-only Graph query suggestion before executing a tenant reporting lookup
avoidWhen: you need a write operation or Exchange configuration change
typicalInputs: how many guest users are in the tenant
tools.returnsSummaryShape: suggested Microsoft Graph queries and explanation for an Entra admin reporting question

question.description: Natural-language tenant admin question to turn into candidate Graph queries.

tools.name: graphenterprise_get
tools.description: Execute a read-only Microsoft Graph request through the Microsoft MCP Server for Enterprise under delegated user context.
useWhen: you already know the read-only Graph path or selected it from a prior query suggestion
avoidWhen: the request would modify tenant state
tools.returnsSummaryShape: raw or structured result of a read-only Microsoft Graph request executed through Enterprise MCP

uri.description: Microsoft Graph request URI or path to execute as a read-only GET.

tools.name: graphenterprise_list_properties
tools.description: List available Microsoft Graph properties and relationships for an entity before forming a tenant report query.
useWhen: you need to inspect an entity schema before building a Graph reporting query
avoidWhen: you already know the required fields and just need to execute the request
tools.returnsSummaryShape: available properties and relationships for the requested Microsoft Graph entity

entity.description: Microsoft Graph entity name to inspect, such as user, group, servicePrincipal, or device.
